confirmed.title: PerformanceMetrics panel empty-state message hardcoded English (not via t())
confirmed.detail:
Visible

empty-state 'No performance metrics captured yet. Interact with the page to populate Web Vitals.' is a raw literal. Every sibling DevTools panel (App/InertiaInspector/PolicyDebugger/FieldsInspector) routes copy through the self-contained t() in src/panel/i18n.ts, but PerformanceMetrics.tsx imports/uses nothing from i18n.ts at all — the entire file is un-localized. Should be t('devtools.performance.empty', '').
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/PerformanceMetrics.tsx:83: the empty-state

renders the raw English literal "No performance metrics captured yet. Interact with the page to populate Web Vitals." with no t() wrapping. This is a visible, user-facing string in the DevTools Performance panel. The whole file is un-localized: it imports nothing from ./i18n. By contrast, the four sibling panels (App.tsx, InertiaInspector.tsx, FieldsInspector.tsx, PolicyDebugger.tsx) all `import { t } from './i18n.js'` and route copy through it. The i18n.ts dictionary contains no devtools.performance.* content keys (only devtools.tab.performance for the tab title), confirming this empty-state message has no localization path. Not a duplicate: the ledger's 13 devtools entries cover TimeTravel.tsx, InertiaInspector.tsx, App.tsx, PolicyDebugger.tsx, and FieldsInspector.tsx — zero entries reference PerformanceMetrics.tsx. Not vendored: file lives in packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. The fix is exactly as proposed: t('devtools.performance.empty', '') plus a dictionary entry (and ideally localizing the rest of the file's literals).

confirmed.title: PerformanceMetrics tile hint 'Cumulative Layout Shift' hardcoded (visible)
confirmed.detail: hint:'Cumulative Layout Shift' rendered visibly via {tile.hint} at line 104. Raw English literal not localized.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against source and ledger. REPRODUCES: At /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/PerformanceMetrics.tsx, the CLS tile is defined with hint: 'Cumulative Layout Shift' (line 66) and that hint is rendered visibly as a {tile.hint} at line 104. The literal is a raw English string with no i18n wrapping. (Note: line cited as 66 for the literal, 104 for the render site — both confirmed.) NOVEL / NOT A DUPLICATE: The i18n-fixed-ledger.json (302 entries, rounds 1-19) contains NO entry for PerformanceMetrics.tsx and no mention of "performance", "Cumulative", or "Layout Shift". Sibling devtools panels are in the ledger (TimeTravel, InertiaInspector, App, PolicyDebugger, FieldsInspector) but PerformanceMetrics was never swept. NOT VENDORED: File lives in packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. It is a first-party authored component. USER-FACING + GENUINE GAP: The devtools extension has an established i18n system — ./i18n.js exports a t(key, fallback) helper, and sibling panels (PolicyDebugger, FieldsInspector, InertiaInspector) all route visible strings through it (e.g. t('devtools.fields.all_types', 'All types')). PerformanceMetrics.tsx imports no i18n and hardcodes ALL visible strings (the four hints incl. 'Cumulative Layout Shift', the empty-state message, server labels 'Queries'/'Memory'). By the project's own convention — which already accepted comparable sibling-panel strings into the ledger — this is a legitimate missing-i18n defect, not an out-of-scope dev tool exclusion. Minor caveat lowering confidence: 'Cumulative Layout Shift' is a standardized Web Vitals metric name and arguably borderline as a translation target, but the same standard already admitted analogous devtools strings, so it qualifies under the project's bar.

confirmed.title: PerformanceMetrics tile hint 'Initial navigation duration' hardcoded (visible)
confirmed.detail: hint:'Initial navigation duration' rendered visibly via {tile.hint} at line 104. Raw English literal not localized.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against the file and the full ledger. REPRODUCES: packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/PerformanceMetrics.tsx has NO translator import/usage (the earlier grep "t(" hit was a false match on `tile.format(...)`). Every string is a raw English literal: line 74 hint 'Initial navigation duration' is defined on the `navigationTime` tile and rendered verbatim at line 104 via `{tile.hint}`. Siblings 'Largest Contentful Paint', 'Interaction latency', 'Cumulative Layout Shift', the empty-state sentence, and 'Queries'/'Memory' are likewise hardcoded. NOT A DUPLICATE: PerformanceMetrics.tsx does not appear anywhere in i18n-fixed-ledger.json (R1-R19). The ledger swept the other devtools-extension panels — App.tsx, InertiaInspector.tsx, PolicyDebugger.tsx, FieldsInspector.tsx (round 19) and TimeTravel.tsx (round 9) — but missed this one. This is the un-swept corner of an already-established family. NOT VENDORED: it is application code under devtools-extension/src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. USER-FACING / IN-SCOPE: the package is a shipped MIT browser DevTools extension (package.json description), and it has a working i18n.ts dictionary with PT-BR translations (e.g. 'devtools.tab.performance' => 'Desempenho'). The round-19 sweep of sibling panels establishes that localizing these panels is the project's intent, so the hardcoded English here is a genuine defect for the developer-user audience. Minor imprecision in the finding: it isolates a single hint while the entire file is un-i18n'd, but the cited string itself genuinely reproduces and is novel.

confirmed.title: PerformanceMetrics tile label 'Navigation' hardcoded (visible)
confirmed.detail: label:'Navigation' rendered visibly via {tile.label} at line 100. Unlike the other tile labels (LCP/INP/FID/CLS) which are acronyms, 'Navigation' is an English word and should be localized.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed real and novel. At /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/PerformanceMetrics.tsx:70 the tile label 'Navigation' is a hardcoded English word, rendered visibly at line 100 via {tile.label}. The component is mounted in the active Performance tab (App.tsx:127), so it is genuinely user-facing, not dead code. The same package ships a working i18n layer (src/panel/i18n.ts exporting t()) which App.tsx uses extensively, including the sibling key t('devtools.tab.performance', 'Performance'); yet PerformanceMetrics.tsx imports no t() at all. The finding's specific framing (that 'Navigation' is uniquely localizable vs. the LCP/INP/FID/CLS acronyms) is slightly narrow — the tile hints ('Largest Contentful Paint', 'Interaction latency', 'Cumulative Layout Shift', 'Initial navigation duration'), the empty-state paragraph, and the 'Queries'/'Memory' server labels are equally un-i18n'd full English strings — but the core defect it cites reproduces exactly. Not a duplicate: the ledger (R1-R19) has zero entries for the devtools-extension package, PerformanceMetrics, tiles, Web Vitals, or navigationTime (the 'Navigation' ledger hits are unrelated nav-package/CommandPalette entries). Not a vendored shadcn primitive — it is a first-party panel component under devtools-extension, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: PerformanceMetrics server counter label 'Queries' hardcoded (visible)
confirmed.detail: Visible <span> text 'Queries' is a raw literal in the server-telemetry footer. Not localized via the panel i18n t().
confirmed.why_real: Verified against source and ledger. REAL: PerformanceMetrics.tsx line 111 has a raw visible literal Queries in the server-telemetry footer. The panel ships a real i18n system at packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/i18n.ts (exported t() with en + pt_BR flat 'devtools.*' dictionaries), and sibling panel components (App, FieldsInspector, InertiaInspector, PolicyDebugger) all import and use t(). PerformanceMetrics.tsx imports nothing from i18n and uses t() nowhere — 'Queries' (111), 'Memory' (115), tile labels, hints, and the empty-state text are all hardcoded English, and the dictionary contains no devtools.performance.* keys. So under a pt_BR locale the footer renders English 'Queries'/'Memory' while the rest of the panel is localized. User-facing (visible footer text in the DevTools panel). Not a vendored shadcn primitive — it lives in packages-js/devtools-extension, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. NOT DUPLICATE: The ledger has 13 devtools-extension entries (TimeTravel, InertiaInspector, App, PolicyDebugger x3, FieldsInspector x4, etc.), with Round 19 sweeping the panel components — but PerformanceMetrics.tsx appears nowhere in rounds 1-19. grep for 'performance'/'Queries'/'PerformanceMetrics' in the ledger returns no match. It is the one panel component that was skipped by the prior i18n passes.

confirmed.title: TimeTravel replay button aria-label 'Replay ${url}' hardcoded English word
confirmed.detail: aria-label={`Replay ${snap.url}`} hardcodes the English verb 'Replay' for the accessible name. Should interpolate a localized string, e.g. t('devtools.time_travel.replay_aria', 'Replay :url', { url: snap.url }). Not in ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/TimeTravel.tsx:118, which reads aria-label={`Replay ${snap.url}`}, hardcoding the English verb "Replay" in the accessible name for the replay button. This is a genuine, user-facing i18n/a11y defect: the accessible name is announced verbatim by screen readers and is not localized. It is first-party code (panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*), so not vendored. Ledger check (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json): the only two TimeTravel.tsx entries are line 82 (toLocaleTimeString locale) and line 56 (ternary pluralization), and the two "line: 118" entries belong to unrelated files (WidgetRenderer.tsx and NavigationCommandProvider.php). No round 1-19 entry covers the Replay aria-label, so it is novel and not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: PerformanceMetrics devtools panel entirely unlocalized (sibling panels were i18n-wired, this one missed)
confirmed.detail: When the devtools-extension panels were localized via the self-contained src/panel/i18n.ts t() helper (ledger covers App/InertiaInspector/PolicyDebugger/FieldsInspector, plus TimeTravel for pluralization), PerformanceMetrics.tsx was skipped: it imports nothing from './i18n' and hardcodes every visible English string. App.tsx (already localized via t()) renders <PerformanceMetrics> live at line 127, so the panel sits inside an otherwise-localized UI but never translates. Hardcoded strings: empty-state notice 'No performance metrics captured yet. Interact with the page to populate Web Vitals.' (line 83), tile hints 'Largest Contentful Paint'/'Interaction latency'/'Cumulative Layout Shift'/'Initial navigation duration' (lines 50,58,66,74), and the server footer labels 'Queries' (line 111) and 'Memory' (line 115). Repro: set navigator.language='pt-BR' (i18n.ts activeLocale() derives from navigator.language), open the Performance tab in the devtools panel; surrounding chrome renders pt_BR but these strings stay English.
confirmed.why_real: Verified against the actual source. packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/PerformanceMetrics.tsx contains zero import statements (confirmed via grep — no './i18n' import) and hardcodes every visible English string: the empty-state notice at line 83 ('No performance metrics captured yet. Interact with the page to populate Web Vitals.'), tile hints at lines 50/58/66/74 ('Largest Contentful Paint', 'Interaction latency', 'Cumulative Layout Shift', 'Initial navigation duration'), and the server footer labels 'Queries' (line 111) and 'Memory' (line 115). It never calls t(). App.tsx imports PerformanceMetrics (line 5) and renders <PerformanceMetrics metrics={...} server={...}/> live at line 127, so the panel sits inside an otherwise-localized devtools UI. The self-contained t() helper exists in i18n.ts with activeLocale() deriving from navigator.language (lines 106-108), so sibling panels translate while this one stays English under pt-BR — a real, user-facing defect. NOT in the ledger: grep of i18n-fixed-ledger.json for the devtools-extension panel directory returns only App, InertiaInspector, PolicyDebugger, FieldsInspector, and TimeTravel (rounds 1-10) — PerformanceMetrics.tsx never appears, matching the finding's own scope claim. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (path is src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/). Genuine, novel, user-facing, not vendored.

confirmed.title: PerformanceMetrics numeric values use toFixed() with hardcoded '.' decimal separator (locale-insensitive)
confirmed.suggested_key: n/a (route through Intl.NumberFormat(activeLocale-bcp47) instead of toFixed)
confirmed.detail: formatMs() (lines 124-128) and formatBytes() (lines 130-133), plus the CLS tile formatter (v) => v.toFixed(3) (line 65), build every displayed metric number with Number.prototype.toFixed(), which always emits a '.' decimal separator and never groups thousands. Under pt-BR the decimal mark must be ',' (e.g. CLS '0,123', not '0.123'; '1,5 MB' not '1.5 MB'; '12,34s' not '12.34s'). Unlike the locale-aware widgets (StatCard/TableCard use new Intl.NumberFormat(locale)), this panel has no access to a locale at all and does not run values through Intl.NumberFormat. The raw {server.queryCount} (line 112) is likewise printed ungrouped. Repro: navigator.language='pt-BR', trigger CLS>0 / navigation>1s; tiles show English-shaped decimals ('0.123', '1.20s') instead of pt-BR ('0,123', '1,20s').
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/PerformanceMetrics.tsx. Claims are factually accurate: line 65 CLS tile uses (v) => v.toFixed(3); formatMs (lines 124-128) uses .toFixed(0/2); formatBytes (lines 130-133) uses .toFixed(1); line 112 prints raw {server.queryCount}. None of these run through Intl.NumberFormat and the component receives no locale prop, so every displayed number uses a hardcoded '.' decimal separator and no thousands grouping. Under navigator.language='pt-BR' the tiles show '0.123'/'1.20s'/'1.5 MB' instead of pt-BR '0,123'/'1,20s'/'1,5 MB'. This reproduces deterministically.

USER-FACING / IN-SCOPE: The project's own ledger treats devtools-extension panel formatting as valid i18n defects — rounds already fixed TimeTravel.tsx and InertiaInspector.tsx toLocaleTimeString-no-locale, plus App.tsx tab labels/empty-state strings. The exact toFixed-decimal-separator family is also an accepted defect class (Checkout, CompareTable, PluginCard, Publisher all flagged for 'locale-insensitive decimal separator (toFixed)'). So 'it is only dev tooling' is not a valid refutation under this project's standard, and the contrast with StatCard/TableCard (which were fixed to use Intl.NumberFormat) confirms the framework's intended locale-aware numeric formatting.

NOT VENDORED: file lives in packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

NOT A DUPLICATE: grep for 'PerformanceMetrics'/'performance' in reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json returns nothing. Prior devtools-extension ledger entries are all different files/lines (TimeTravel.tsx, InertiaInspector.tsx, PolicyDebugger.tsx, FieldsInspector.tsx, App.tsx). Rounds 1-19 never touched PerformanceMetrics.tsx or its toFixed/queryCount formatting. Novel.
